intro:
I couldn't find any information on "Khadia beswickii". However, I found a plant called "Khadira" or "Acacia catechu" (also known as Khadia Beswickii is a synonym for this plant), which is a plant species in the legume family. 

Acacia catechu, or Khadia beswickii, is a plant that is known for its medicinal properties and is used as a medicinal plant in traditional Ayurvedic medicine. The health benefits of Acacia catechu include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to help with digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Acacia catechu include catechin, epicatechin, and other flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Acacia catechu include a paste or powder made from the heartwood, which is used to treat various health conditions.
